visual: Two multiple choice options saying "Row" and "Column".
guide: "Last challenge. I want an array with 20 dots total. You choose: build it with rows first or columns first, pick your numbers, and find the equation. There's more than one right answer."
prompt: "Choose rows or columns to build an array with 20 dots."
visual_2: On valid specification, the buttons activate (matches whichever the student named, "Rows & Items per Row" or "Columns & Items per Column").
student_action_2: Student builds first dimension (placeholders) and second dimension (dots fill in at intersections).
visual_3: Equation Builder words mode. The array from previous step still on the screen.
guide_2: "You built it. Now write the word description for your array".
correct_state_2: Any valid specification that matches the row or column array which = 20 (e.g., "4 rows of 5," "5 columns of 4," "2 rows of 10," "10 rows of 2".).
visual_4: Equation Builder equation mode. The array and the word equation from the previous step still on the screen.
guide_3: "Now write the equation. What multiplication does your array show?"
correct_answer: Equation must match the word mode description AND product must equal 20.
on_correct: (example: 4 × 5 = 20) "4 times 5 equals 20. You chose 4 rows of 5. That's one way to build 20."
on_correct_2: (example: 2 × 10 = 20) "10 times 2 equals 20.  10 columns of 2. That's an array with 20 dots."
